sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Apple’s WWDC event goes big on design, but says less on AI
feed.summary: At its annual Worldwide Developers Conference (WWDC), Apple primarily showcased a new "Liquid Glass" interface for its products, emphasizing design over artificial intelligence. While the company introduced an app for games and a live translation feature, it offered limited updates on its AI initiatives, acknowledging delays in promised Siri advancements. Apple also announced it would open its "Apple Intelligence" AI model to developers, but analysts noted the company's relatively slow pace in AI compared to competitors, alongside other challenges like tariff threats and regulatory hurdles.

feed.title: iPadOS 26 introduces powerful new features that push the capabilities and versatility of iPad even further
feed.summary: Apple previewed iPadOS 26, calling it the "biggest iPadOS release ever," which brings significant advancements to the iPad experience. Key features include a redesigned interface with Liquid Glass, an entirely new intuitive windowing system, and enhanced integration of Apple Intelligence for improved communication and creativity. The update also introduces new capabilities for file management, audio/video professionals, and new apps like Journal and the Apple Games app.

feed.title: Apple elevates the iPhone experience with iOS 26
feed.summary: Apple previewed iOS 26, a major update featuring a new "Liquid Glass" design and powerful Apple Intelligence capabilities. Key enhancements include Live Translation, visual intelligence for screen interactions, and new ways to express oneself with Genmoji and Image Playground. The update also brings significant improvements to Phone, Messages, CarPlay, Apple Music, Maps, Wallet, and introduces a new Apple Games app.

feed.title: North America Consumer Electronics Market Size, Share, 2033
feed.summary: The North America Consumer Electronics Market was valued at USD 245.11 billion in 2024 and is projected to reach USD 369.62 billion by 2033, growing at a CAGR of 4.67% from 2025 to 2033. Key drivers include rising disposable incomes, urbanization, and advancements in 5G and IoT technologies, while challenges involve supply chain disruptions, rapid technological obsolescence, and intense competition from budget brands. Smartphones dominate product categories, and retail stores remain the primary distribution channel, though e-commerce is rapidly expanding.

feed.title: Market Forecast News: Wearable Healthcare Devices Industry to Hit $75.98B by 2030
feed.summary: The wearable healthcare devices market is projected to reach USD 75.98 billion by 2030 from USD 45.29 billion in 2025, growing at a CAGR of 10.9%. This growth is driven by rising health consciousness, technological advancements, and an increasing geriatric population, despite challenges like inconsistent regulations and data security concerns. Smartwatches and consumer-grade devices are leading the market segments, with North America holding the largest share due to its advanced infrastructure and high smartphone penetration.

feed.title: Introducing Logitech Muse for Apple Vision Pro
feed.summary: Logitech has announced Muse for Apple Vision Pro, a digital pencil designed to enhance work, creation, and collaboration experiences within spatial computing. Muse offers precision, natural interaction, and features like fluid motion tracking, 6 degrees of freedom tracking, haptic response, and pressure-sensing tips. This new device aims to transform creative workflows and productivity, and was featured in Apple's WWDC25 keynote.

feed.title: India’s first Aatmanirbhar semiconductor chip is finally here!
feed.summary: India is set to roll out its first indigenously produced semiconductor chips by mid-2025, marking a significant step towards self-reliance in technology. Union IT Minister Ashwini Vaishnaw confirmed that 28nm and 90nm nodes will begin production, a crucial development for India despite not being cutting-edge global technology. This initiative aims to establish India's credibility in chip manufacturing, foster a domestic supply chain for essential electronics, and reduce dependence on foreign imports and geopolitical risks.

feed.title: Qualcomm to acquire Alphawave Semi for $2.4B
feed.summary: Qualcomm has agreed to acquire Alphawave Semi for $2.4 billion, aiming to strengthen its position in the artificial intelligence and data center markets. This acquisition is part of Qualcomm's broader strategy to enhance its product capabilities for next-generation technologies and expand into new markets. The deal is expected to close in the first quarter of 2026 and will provide Qualcomm with Alphawave's high-speed connectivity and compute silicon assets.

feed.title: Serviços Apple oferecem recursos poderosos e atualizações inteligentes ainda este ano
feed.summary: A Apple anunciou uma série de novos recursos e atualizações inteligentes para seus serviços, que serão lançados com o iOS 26 e outros sistemas operacionais ainda este ano. As novidades incluem AutoMix no Apple Music, rotas favoritas e Locais Visitados no Mapas da Apple, rastreamento de pedidos com Apple Intelligence na Carteira da Apple, e a introdução da Identidade digital. Essas atualizações visam tornar o dia a dia dos usuários mais prático, divertido e personalizado.

feed.title: Wearing a computer on your face? Snap looks to take on rivals with new augmented reality glasses
feed.summary: Snap, the company behind Snapchat, plans to release new augmented reality (AR) glasses, known as Specs, in 2026, aiming to move computing beyond smartphones and into the real world. This initiative comes as tech giants like Meta, Google, and Apple are also heavily investing in AR glasses and AI-powered wearables. Despite fierce competition and past commercial struggles with smart glasses, Snap is banking on its $3 billion investment and 11 years of development to reshape how people interact with technology and diversify its revenue streams beyond digital advertising.

feed.title: Hiring confidence steady in India for July–September; IT, energy and BFSI to lead recruitment push, says ManpowerGroup employment outlook survey
feed.summary: Corporate India expects robust hiring for July-September 2025, driven by strong employer confidence in IT, energy, and financial sectors. The Net Employment Outlook stands at 42%, ranking second globally after the UAE, and marks a significant year-on-year increase. Despite global trade headwinds, companies are increasing investment in automation and adapting their workforce strategies to meet new skill demands, with Energy & Utilities, IT, and Financial Services showing the strongest outlooks.

feed.title: Insulet’s Omnipod® 5 App for iPhone with Dexcom G7 Integration Now Available to all U.S. Users
feed.summary: Insulet has announced that its Omnipod® 5 App for iPhone now integrates with the Dexcom G7 Continuous Glucose Monitoring (CGM) System, allowing all U.S. users to manage diabetes directly from their iPhone. This integration aims to simplify diabetes management by offering convenient control of insulin delivery and glucose monitoring through a single device. The Omnipod 5 System automatically adjusts insulin delivery and helps maintain stable glucose levels, enhancing user experience and choice in diabetes care.

feed.title: Travel eSIM provider Kolet attracts investment from Expedia, Apple vets
feed.summary: French travel eSIM provider Kolet has successfully raised $10 million in Series A funding, with significant investment from Daphni, former Expedia Group CEO Peter Kern, and Apple's former VP of marketing Jon Gieselman. Kolet, founded in 2024, offers digital eSIMs and has already partnered with major travel brands like Air France, KLM, and Kiwi.com. The new capital will be utilized to improve its mobile app user experience, simplify eSIM installation, and expand its team by hiring 10 new tech, product, and business employees.

feed.title: Snap Plans to Launch New Consumer ‘Specs’ AR Glasses Next Year
feed.summary: Snap, the company behind Snapchat, announced plans to release a new consumer version of its Spectacles AR glasses (aka 'Specs') in 2026. This updated version is teased to have a smaller form factor, lighter weight, and increased capabilities compared to the developer-focused Spectacles '24. Significant updates to Snap OS will also be introduced, including integrations with OpenAI and Google Cloud's Gemini to enable multimodal AI-powered Lenses.

feed.title: Why Investors Shouldn't Be Surprised By Liberty Global Ltd.'s (NASDAQ:LBTY.A) Low P/S
feed.summary: Liberty Global Ltd. (NASDAQ:LBTY.A) has a low price-to-sales (P/S) ratio of 0.8x compared to the industry average of 1.4x, which might signal a buying opportunity. However, this low P/S is attributed to analyst forecasts of limited future revenue growth (1.9% per annum) that significantly underperform the broader telecom industry's anticipated 102% annual expansion, making investors unwilling to pay a higher premium for the stock. While recent revenue growth has been strong, historical three-year performance showed a significant decline, contributing to the cautious market sentiment.

feed.title: What Are Equal-Weight Index Funds?
feed.summary: Equal-weight index funds hold assets in identical proportions, differing from cap-weighted funds that prioritize larger companies. While they offer benefits like reduced concentration risk and a value-minded approach, they tend to have higher expense ratios and can be more volatile. Despite underperforming cap-weighted funds in recent years due to the dominance of mega-cap tech stocks, equal-weighting can be attractive to investors concerned about over-concentration in large companies.

feed.title: This Gen X CEO runs Eventbrite, the ticketing platform that has sold over two billion tickets—she loves Legos, hypnotherapy, and skin-care gadgets
feed.summary: This article profiles Julia Hartz, co-founder and CEO of Eventbrite, detailing her career trajectory from MTV and FX to leading a global ticketing platform. It explores her personal interests, financial habits, and work-life balance while managing a successful company and raising four daughters. The piece offers a glimpse into how a C-suite executive spends her time and money outside of work, including her love for Legos, hypnotherapy, and innovative skin-care gadgets.

feed.title: How to Buy Vanguard S&P 500 ETF (VOO)
feed.summary: This guide details how to invest in the Vanguard S&P 500 ETF (VOO), one of the largest and most popular ETFs, known for its low expense ratio (0.03%) and its goal to match the S&P 500 index returns. It covers VOO's holdings, reasons to invest or not invest, its dividend payments, and historical performance. The article also provides a step-by-step guide to buying shares through a brokerage account.

feed.title: Big movements in NuCana plc stock price on Wednesday moving 32.83% between high and low
feed.summary: The NuCana plc (NCNA) stock price surged by 14.98% on Wednesday, June 11, 2025, continuing a four-day gaining streak. Despite a positive short-term Moving Average signal and a recent pivot bottom buy signal, the stock faces a general sell signal from its long-term Moving Average and is considered extremely overbought on RSI14. Analysts from William Blair downgraded NCNA to "Market Perform" in August 2024, and the stock is anticipated to perform weakly in the near future due to high volatility and conflicting technical signals.

feed.title: QQDN Stock Price, News & Analysis
feed.summary: The article provides an overview of ProShares UltraShort QQQ Mega (QQDN), an ETF designed to deliver -2x the daily performance of the Nasdaq-100 Mega Index. It details the fund's investment objective, benchmark components (large Nasdaq-100 companies like Apple, Microsoft, NVIDIA), and the use of derivatives. The report also highlights significant risks associated with geared ETFs, including potential deviations from the daily target over longer holding periods, volatility, and sector concentration.

feed.title: Berkshire Hathaway top holdings: Warren Buffett’s 2025 portfolio
feed.summary: This article details Berkshire Hathaway's top 15 holdings as of Q1 2025, based on its latest SEC filing, as Warren Buffett prepares to step down by the end of 2025. It highlights significant portfolio movements, including a full exit from Citigroup and increased investment in Constellation Brands, and discusses each major holding’s performance and recent financial results. The article also provides a comprehensive list of all 36 publicly listed US equities held by Berkshire Hathaway and outlines trading strategies for investing in these stocks.

feed.title: ProShares Launches First ETFs to Target 2x and -2x Daily Returns of Nasdaq-100 Mega Index
feed.summary: ProShares has launched two new ETFs, ProShares Ultra Top QQQ (QQUP) and ProShares UltraShort Top QQQ (QQDN), which are the first to target 2x and -2x daily returns of the Nasdaq-100 Mega Index, respectively. This index focuses on a concentrated group of the largest Nasdaq-100 companies, including giants like Apple and Microsoft, representing about 45% of the Nasdaq-100. The launch aims to provide investors with leveraged or short exposure to these top tech companies through a single ETF trade, further expanding ProShares' leadership in leveraged and inverse ETFs.

feed.title: Global Partners Debuts All-In-One Digital Experience
feed.summary: Global Partners LP has launched Bee's Knees Benefits, a new mobile app and loyalty program designed to integrate and enhance the digital experience across its various brands like Alltown Fresh, Alltown, and ExtraMart. The program offers exclusive promotions, personalized rewards, mobile ordering, and in-app fuel payments. It aims to provide a consistent and elevated guest experience, consolidating previous loyalty programs into a single platform powered by Paytronix and built on the Rovertown platform.

feed.title: Why Apple, Microsoft, Pfizer and other U.S. multinational corporations set up shop in Ireland
feed.summary: U.S. corporations like Apple, Microsoft, and Pfizer established operations in Ireland, leveraging policies such as the "double Irish" to reduce their corporate income tax rates significantly. While Ireland has revised its tax codes and raised its corporate income tax rate, many U.S. businesses continue to operate there due to an educated workforce, access to European markets, and historical relationships. The U.S. government has expressed a desire for these activities to return to American soil.

feed.title: Pillar Media and AIM Bring Radio to the TV
feed.summary: Pillar Media has partnered with All in Media (AIM), a subsidiary of Xperi, to launch TV apps for its four Christian radio stations. These apps are now available on Roku, Apple TV, Android TV, and Fire TV, allowing listeners to access simulcast audio and view on-air host profiles. This move was prompted by listener inquiries and a Jacobs Media survey indicating high Smart TV ownership among their audience.

feed.title: ProShares Ultra Top QQQ ETF Price, Holdings, & News (NASDAQ:QQUP)
feed.summary: This article provides comprehensive information on the ProShares Ultra Top QQQ ETF (NASDAQ:QQUP), which aims to deliver 2x the daily performance of the Nasdaq-100 Mega Index. It details the ETF's current price, key statistics, holdings, expense ratios, and recent news headlines. The fund is highlighted as a short-term trading tool for amplified exposure to mega-cap tech stocks, not a long-term investment.

feed.title: Spotify Technology Stock Outlook: Will Profitability Hit a Sustained High Note?
feed.summary: Spotify's Q1 2025 results indicate a significant shift towards profitability, with increased revenue, gross profit, and free cash flow, driven by pricing changes and cost management. The company, valued at $140 billion, relies on its loyal user base and personalized listening experience to fend off competition from tech giants and navigate content cost challenges. Despite risks like licensing fees and market saturation, Spotify aims to turn recent profitability into a sustained trend by deepening engagement and expanding monetization.

feed.title: Apple Opens iOS 26 Lock Screen Animation To Spotify And Other Third-Party Music Apps: Report
feed.summary: Apple is reportedly extending its new iOS 26 lock screen feature, which displays animated album art behind music playback controls, to third-party music apps such as Spotify and Audible. This change, enabled through a new API called MPMediaItemAnimatedArtwork, will allow developers to provide animated visuals on the lock screen, moving beyond Apple Music's exclusivity. While developers can access these tools now, end users will see the feature enabled in third-party apps once iOS 26 officially launches in the fall, though it remains uncertain if Spotify will adopt it immediately.

feed.title: Will autonomous vehicles really slash insurance costs?
feed.summary: This article examines the complex relationship between the rise of autonomous vehicles (AVs) and the future of auto insurance. While some projections, like Goldman Sachs', suggest a potential 50% drop in per-mile insurance costs by 2040 due to reduced human error, the immediate future presents challenges such as rising repair costs for high-tech vehicles and new liabilities related to software and cyber threats. The article highlights the ongoing struggles with liability, fragmented regulation, and the slow progression towards fully autonomous driving, emphasizing that insurers must adapt their models to new risk taxonomies.

feed.title: Apple supplier Jabil plans $500M venture for AI infrastructure
feed.summary: Jabil, an electronics component maker and Apple supplier, is investing $500 million over the next several years to expand its cloud and artificial intelligence data center infrastructure in the Southeastern U.S. This investment includes large-scale production facilities expected to be operational by mid-2026. The move aligns with similar U.S. infrastructure investments by tech giants like Apple, Nvidia, and Foxconn, aimed at strengthening domestic manufacturing and diversifying Jabil's commercial portfolio to meet surging AI infrastructure demand.

feed.title: New Buffett-Inspired ETF Holds Berkshire and Apple. It Aims for 15% Yield.
feed.summary: A new income-oriented ETF, VistaShares Target 15 Berkshire Select Income ETF, inspired by Warren Buffett's equity portfolio, has garnered nearly $250 million. Since its launch in March, the fund has provided investors with a 15% distribution rate. It holds 21 stocks, including Berkshire Hathaway and Apple.

feed.title: Texas Instruments injects $60B into U.S. chip manufacturing
feed.summary: Texas Instruments announced a $60 billion plan to bolster domestic semiconductor manufacturing across seven facilities in Utah and Texas, marking the largest effort by a single U.S. company. This investment aims to create a domestic supply chain for chips used in various electronics and is expected to generate 60,000 new U.S. jobs through partnerships with companies like Nvidia, Apple, Ford, and SpaceX. While the initiative is substantial for the semiconductor supply, industry analysts note that realizing gains from increased manufacturing will take time.

feed.title: Texas Instruments commits $60B to domestic chip manufacturing
feed.summary: Texas Instruments plans to invest over $60 billion in domestic chip manufacturing, building seven fabrication facilities across Texas and Utah. This initiative, supported by $1.6 billion in CHIPS Act funding, aims to produce foundational semiconductors for major tech and automotive companies like Apple, Ford, and Nvidia. The investment addresses rising compute demand driven by AI adoption and geopolitical considerations to boost U.S. chip production.

feed.title: Tech Tactically Trimmed: Why I'm Holding MAGS But With Caution
feed.summary: The author remains bullish on big tech long-term but advises caution due to current Magnificent 7 valuations and macro headwinds. While the MAGS ETF offers a unique hybrid structure, its performance can be replicated with a DIY approach. Valuations are stretched for some names, but Microsoft, Google, Apple, and Amazon still show upside potential, leading the author to rate MAGS a Hold, preferring QQQ for now.

feed.title: Should iShares S&P 500 Value ETF (IVE) Be on Your Investing Radar?
feed.summary: The iShares S&P 500 Value ETF (IVE) offers broad exposure to the Large Cap Value segment of the US equity market, launched in 2000 and managed by Blackrock with over $38 billion in assets. It maintains a low expense ratio of 0.18% and has a Zacks ETF Rank of 2 (Buy), making it a suitable option for investors seeking exposure to large-cap value stocks. The ETF primarily invests in Information Technology, Financials, and Healthcare sectors, with Apple, Microsoft, and Amazon as top holdings, offering diversification and medium risk.

feed.title: Texas Instruments Partners With Apple, NVIDIA in Historic $60B US Semiconductor Expansion
feed.summary: Texas Instruments (TXN) announced a historic investment of over $60 billion in seven U.S. semiconductor fabs across three mega-sites in Texas and Utah. This expansion, the largest of its kind in U.S. history, will create more than 60,000 U.S. jobs and produce foundational analog and embedded processing chips essential for various electronic devices. TI has also strengthened partnerships with major companies like Apple, Ford, Medtronic, NVIDIA, and SpaceX, showcasing a commitment to securing domestic supply chains and advancing American innovation.

feed.title: Texas Instruments plans to invest more than $60 billion to manufacture billions of foundational semiconductors in the U.S.
feed.summary: Texas Instruments announced a plan to invest over $60 billion in U.S. semiconductor fabs, the largest such investment in U.S. history, to expand manufacturing capacity for foundational semiconductors. This initiative, supported by partnerships with major companies like Apple, Ford, Medtronic, NVIDIA, and SpaceX, aims to create over 60,000 U.S. jobs and advance American innovation in critical technologies. The investment will build seven large-scale fabs across three mega-sites in Texas and Utah, significantly increasing the domestic supply of essential chips.

feed.title: California men charged in $100M jewelry heist involving Brinks truck, largest jewelry heist in US history
feed.summary: Seven men from California have been charged in connection with the theft of $100 million worth of jewelry from a Brink's semi-truck in Lebec, California, in 2022. The Department of Justice considers this the largest jewelry heist in U.S. history. The suspects scouted the truck after an international jewelry show and followed it before stealing 24 bags of valuables.

The piece highlights the opportunities and risks involved in DAX trading, emphasizing the importance of risk management, and promotes XTB's platform for its competitive spreads, robust tools, and customer support.

feed.title: Texas Instruments to Invest $60bn in US Semiconductors

This move aims to strengthen critical domestic supply chains, support over 60,000 US jobs, and enable innovation across various industries. Major US manufacturers like Apple, Ford, Medtronic, NVIDIA, and SpaceX are collaborating with TI, emphasizing the importance of this investment for US manufacturing and technological advancement.

feed.title: Texas Instruments plans to invest $60B in semiconductor fabs
feed.summary: Texas Instruments announced a massive $60 billion investment into seven new semiconductor fabrication plants across Texas and Utah. This significant expansion will boost U.S. manufacturing capacity for 300mm chips used by major companies like Apple, Ford, and Nvidia. The initiative is partly supported by $1.6 billion from the CHIPS and Science Act, aiming to solidify the U.S.'s position in the global semiconductor market.

feed.title: Texas Instruments To Invest Over $60B to Manufacture 'Billions' of Semiconductors in North Texas and Utah
feed.summary: Texas Instruments plans to invest over $60 billion in expanding its U.S. semiconductor manufacturing capacity across seven fabs in North Texas and Utah. This significant investment aims to bolster the domestic supply chain for foundational semiconductors vital for various electronics and is expected to create more than 60,000 U.S. jobs. The initiative is supported by the Trump administration and collaborates with major companies like Apple, Ford, Medtronic, NVIDIA, and SpaceX, all of whom rely on TI's technology.

feed.title: 4 Diversified ETFs That Send You a Check Every Month (No Stock Picking Needed)
feed.summary: This article highlights four diversified Exchange Traded Funds (ETFs) that provide monthly income to investors without requiring individual stock picking. It presents two tech-focused funds, JPMorgan Nasdaq Equity Premium Income ETF (JEPQ) and NEOS NASDAQ-100 High Income ETF (QQQI), which offer high yields and exposure to large-cap technology stocks. Additionally, it introduces two multi-sector ETFs, Amplify CWP Enhanced Dividend Income ETF (DIVO) and NEOS S&P 500 High Income ETF (SPYI), that provide broader diversification across various industries, balancing yield with safety.

feed.title: Forget Monthly Dividends, These 5 ETFs Pay Investors Every Week
feed.summary: This article highlights several ETFs that offer weekly dividend payments, catering to investors seeking regular income. It details specific ETFs like AAPL WeeklyPay (AAPW), NVDA WeeklyPay (NVDW), PLTR WeeklyPay (PLTW), TSLA WeeklyPay (TSLW), and the Weekly T-Bill ETF (WEEK), outlining their expense ratios, distribution rates, and underlying investments, which often include swap agreements and major tech stocks. The article also mentions the YieldMax AI & Tech Portfolio Option Income ETF (GPTY) as another option for weekly income.

feed.title: Accenture is worst-performing stock in the S&P 500 on Friday
feed.summary: Accenture became the worst-performing stock in the S&P 500 on Friday, despite reporting strong revenue and profits, along with optimistic guidance. The decline was attributed to investors' concerns over a 6% decrease in third-quarter bookings, which totaled $19.7 billion. Shares of Accenture PLC Class A were down 8.2% and on pace for their biggest drop since March 2024, having fallen 20% year-to-date.

feed.title: What If You Invested In S&P 500 Excluding Mag 7? Here's How NVDA, AMZN, AAPL And Others Have Performed In The Last Year - Apple (NASDAQ:AAPL), Amazon.com (NASDAQ:AMZN)
feed.summary: The S&P 500's growth over the last decade has been almost entirely driven by the "Magnificent 7" stocks. A Goldman Sachs study indicates that excluding these seven companies, the remaining 493 S&P 500 stocks showed negligible returns, highlighting the concentrated nature of market gains. Despite their long-term dominance, only Nvidia, Microsoft, and Meta Platforms have outperformed the broader S&P 500 year-to-date in 2025 among the Magnificent 7.

feed.title: Texas Instruments to Put $60 Billion Toward Semiconductor Manufacturing in North Texas
feed.summary: Texas Instruments plans to invest over $60 billion in semiconductor manufacturing, establishing "mega-sites" in Sherman, Richardson, and Lehi, Utah, which is expected to create over 60,000 jobs. This investment is supported by the Trump administration to boost American manufacturing and involves partnerships with major companies like Apple, Ford, Medtronic, NVIDIA, and SpaceX. Additionally, the article covers BGSF's sale of its professional division for $99 million and the upcoming opening of Flight Club, a social entertainment concept, in Addison.

feed.title: This Nasdaq ETF Could Turn $500 Monthly Into $1 Million
feed.summary: The article discusses how consistently investing $500 monthly into the Invesco QQQ ETF (NASDAQ: QQQ), which tracks the Nasdaq-100, could potentially lead to accumulating $1 million. It highlights the ETF's historical returns, driven by its heavy weighting in large-cap tech stocks, and provides a projection of investment growth over various timeframes. The author also advises using QQQ as a supplemental part of a portfolio due to its concentration in the tech sector.

feed.title: LG pOLED: introduction and market status
feed.summary: This article introduces LG Display's pOLED technology, which stands for plastic OLED and refers to their flexible AMOLED displays for mobile devices. It details LG's production history, including their move into smartphone displays in 2017 and their current production facilities. The article also clarifies the distinction between LGD's pOLED branding and PLED (Polymer-based OLED materials).

feed.title: The Vanguard Growth ETF Is a Great Choice for Most, But I Like the Invesco QQQ Trust Better
feed.summary: This article compares the Vanguard Growth ETF (VUG) and the Invesco QQQ Trust (QQQ), highlighting that while both are strong growth-focused ETFs, QQQ has shown better historical performance and a less top-heavy portfolio. The author expresses a preference for QQQ due to these factors but emphasizes that consistent investing through dollar-cost averaging is crucial for long-term wealth creation with either ETF.

feed.title: Texas Instruments plans to invest $60B in semiconductor fabs
feed.summary: Texas Instruments announced plans to invest over $60 billion in seven semiconductor fabrication plants across Texas and Utah, marking one of the largest investments in the sector. This investment will significantly increase U.S. manufacturing capacity for 300mm chips, supported by $1.6 billion in funding from the CHIPS and Science Act, and will create more than 60,000 new jobs. The new fabs will produce chips for major companies like Apple, Ford, Medtronic, and Nvidia, enhancing the country's position in advanced semiconductor technology.

feed.title: DeFi Development Corp. Partners with Kraken to Tokenize
feed.summary: DeFi Development Corp. (DFDV) has strategically partnered with Kraken to tokenize its stock on the Solana blockchain, making it the first U.S.-listed crypto treasury strategy company to trade on-chain. This initiative is part of Kraken's xStocks alliance and aims to bridge traditional capital markets with the Solana ecosystem, allowing for new financial product development and broader participation in public equity markets. Tokenized shares will trade as DFDVx, offering developers and institutions a "DeFi lego block" to innovate with equity ownership and on-chain finance.

feed.title: IWF Is a Great Choice for Most, But I Like VUG ETF Better
feed.summary: The article compares two growth-focused ETFs, the iShares Russell 1000 Growth ETF (IWF) and the Vanguard Growth ETF (VUG). While both offer similar top holdings, VUG is favored due to its significantly lower expense ratio and historically better returns across most periods. The author recommends VUG for investors seeking to add growth to their portfolios due to its cost-efficiency and superior performance.

feed.title: Delta Air Lines Just Got Some Very Good News. The Hard Part? Doing it All Over Again
feed.summary: Delta Air Lines has been ranked the best U.S. airline for the seventh consecutive year by The Points Guy, an impressive feat in a competitive industry. The article highlights that Delta's success stems from consistently performing well across various metrics, rather than excelling in just one area. This consistent high performance provides a strong takeaway for any business aiming for sustained success.

feed.title: Moomoo Malaysia moves from free stock dispenser to indispensable investment platform
feed.summary: Moomoo Securities Malaysia, having attracted over 500,000 sign-ups since its launch in February last year through aggressive giveaways, is now shifting its strategy to become a lasting force in Malaysia's investment landscape. CEO Ivan Mok emphasizes the platform's goal to drive fintech infrastructure, enhance market efficiency, and increase local participation in capital markets, envisioning Moomoo Malaysia as an indispensable part of the country's financial future within five years. The company plans to continue integrating promotional strategies with a focus on innovative technology, real-time data, professional analytical tools, and educational experiences to empower Malaysian investors.

feed.title: IDC: Global Wrist-Worn Device Shipments Grew 10.5% in Q1 2025
feed.summary: Global wrist-worn device shipments increased by 10.5% year-over-year in Q1 2025, reaching 45.6 million units, driven by strong recovery in key regions and government subsidies in China. Huawei led the global market, followed by Xiaomi and Apple, with significant growth seen especially in wristbands and smartwatches in China. IDC predicts continued growth for the Chinese wrist-worn market, reaching 58.5 million units in 2025 due to health initiatives and ongoing subsidies.

feed.title: JD.com Named to Gartner’s Global Supply Chain Top 25 for Second Consecutive Year
feed.summary: JD.com has been recognized on Gartner's Supply Chain Top 25 for the second consecutive year, ranking 22nd globally, making it the only Chinese retailer on the list. This achievement highlights the company's significant investment in R&D, advanced technological implementations like autonomous operations and AI in logistics, and its commitment to building a green supply chain. JD.com's focus on efficiency, customer satisfaction with 24-hour delivery for 95% of orders in China, and its sustainable practices were key factors in this prestigious recognition.

feed.title: Duolingo director hacker sells $4.7m in stock
feed.summary: Severin Hacker, Duolingo's Chief Tech Officer and Director, sold 9,000 shares of Class A Common Stock for approximately $4.7 million on June 20, 2025, while also exercising options to acquire 10,000 shares. This comes as Duolingo's stock experienced a decline due to Apple's new Live Translation feature, though analysts like JPMorgan, DA Davidson, and Scotiabank have raised price targets citing strong user growth and monetization strategies.

feed.title: I tested the Sonos Ace for a year, and you won't want to miss this massive deal
feed.summary: The Sonos Ace headphones, though not perfect and initially priced high at $449, are highly recommended due to a significant Black Friday deal that slashes their price to $332.29. The author, who has tested them for a year, highlights their industry-leading comfort, solid sound quality, and effective active noise canceling as key reasons to consider them, especially now that they are more competitively priced than alternatives like Sony, Bose, and Apple's offerings. While ROE is a key metric, the report advises considering other factors like profit growth rates and stock valuation.

feed.title: News | US tech companies renew push to own their office real estate
feed.summary: Global tech companies like Apple, LinkedIn, Amazon, and Bet365 are increasingly purchasing their office real estate instead of leasing, closing deals totaling roughly $1 billion. This trend is driven by a desire for greater control over their space, the ability to capitalize on discounted properties in a stressed market, and a strategic shift back to ownership post-pandemic. The move is helping to fill a market gap left by less active institutional investors and signals a renewed commitment to physical office spaces by these tech giants.

feed.title: Apple reportedly bids $150M for annual F1 streaming rights in US
feed.summary: Apple is reportedly bidding over $150 million annually for the exclusive US streaming rights to Formula One, starting in 2026. This aggressive bid positions Apple as the frontrunner, potentially outbidding current broadcaster ESPN, whose contract expires this season. The move underscores Apple's strategic push to expand its live sports streaming presence on Apple TV+, following its previous deals with Major League Soccer and Major League Baseball.

feed.title: US stock market future slips as Trump’s 30% tariffs rattle global trade; Dow, S&P 500, Nasdaq dip while Bo
feed.summary: US stock futures slipped after President Donald Trump announced 30% tariffs on imports from the EU and Mexico, beginning August 1st, stoking global trade tensions. Investors are also closely watching second-quarter earnings reports from major banks, upcoming inflation data, and the growing rift between Trump and Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell. Despite the market dip, Boeing saw gains while Apple and Tesla slid, and Bitcoin hit a new record high.

feed.title: Mattel Tries AI Pairing
feed.summary: Mattel announced a partnership with OpenAI to use generative AI for content creation with its iconic brands like Barbie, Hot Wheels, and Uno. This collaboration marks OpenAI's first venture into the toymaking and childhood entertainment sector. Mattel's chief franchise officer, Josh Silverman, stated that AI will help expand the reach of their brands and reimagine new forms of play, building on Mattel's strategy to diversify its intellectual property into various entertainment forms.

feed.title: MP Materials and Apple Announce $500 Million Partnership to Produce Recycled Rare Earth Magnets in the United States
feed.summary: MP Materials announced a $500 million long-term partnership with Apple to supply rare earth magnets made from 100 percent recycled materials. This agreement will enable MP Materials to expand its Fort Worth, Texas, factory and establish a dedicated recycling line at its Mountain Pass site, providing Apple with domestically sourced magnets for its products starting in 2027. The collaboration aims to strengthen U.S. manufacturing, advance circular supply chains, and reduce waste by recovering rare earth elements from recycled materials.

feed.title: Meet the new Nextdoor: more useful, more helpful, and more timely
feed.summary: Nextdoor Holdings, Inc. (NYSE: KIND) has released a significant redesign of its platform, focusing on three core features: Alerts, News, and Faves. This update aims to make the Nextdoor experience more useful, helpful, and timely for its 100 million-plus users. The redesign includes real-time alerts for safety and preparedness, integrated local news from trusted sources, and an AI-powered "Faves" feature for personalized recommendations.

feed.title: Rare Earth Royalty: Meet The Power Players Shaping The Industry
feed.summary: This article identifies five key companies shaping the rare earth metals industry, driven by global demand for critical minerals in clean energy and high-tech sectors. It highlights MP Materials, USA Rare Earth, NioCorp, TMC the metals company, and American Resources, detailing their market capitalization, headquarters, focus, and recent developments that position them as significant players in the evolving rare earth market.

feed.title: Tetra Pak family pours US$9 billion into stocks
feed.summary: The Rausing family, founders of Tetra Pak, has built a stock portfolio worth approximately $9 billion, spread across over 100 companies in Europe and the US. Their investments are primarily held through entities in Liechtenstein, Singapore, and Switzerland, with significant stakes in companies like International Flavors & Fragrances Inc., Linde Plc, and Givaudan SA. This significant, previously unreported portfolio highlights the increasing sophistication of wealth management among ultra-rich families.

feed.title: ASML's stock price is dropping today after the semiconductor giant issued this critical warning about 2026
feed.summary: ASML's stock price dropped significantly after the company reported Q2 2025 results that beat expectations but issued a cautious outlook for 2026. CEO Christophe Fouquet stated that while they prepare for growth in 2026, they cannot confirm it due to increasing uncertainties from macroeconomic and geopolitical developments, including potential tariffs. This warning overshadowed strong financial performance and a 15% growth forecast for 2025, leading to a premarket stock decline.

feed.title: Vornado Lands $450M To Refinance Penn 11
feed.summary: Vornado Realty Trust has secured a $450M CMBS loan to refinance its 1.2M SF office tower, Penn 11, located near Penn Station in Midtown South. The five-year, interest-only loan has a fixed rate of 6.35% and will mature in August 2030. Vornado contributed an additional $50M in equity to pay off the existing $500M loan, demonstrating its continued investment in the Penn Station area redevelopment despite market challenges.

feed.title: Whatever Happened to the Broadening Stock Market Rally?
feed.summary: The stock market rally is becoming increasingly concentrated among a small group of leading stocks, primarily in the tech sector, defying earlier expectations for broader participation. While the US Large-Mid Index reached new highs in July, most stocks are trading significantly below their 52-week highs, with technology driving a large portion of the gains. This concentration raises questions about whether lagging stocks will "catch up" or if leading stocks will "catch down" through a correction, with analysts offering different views on the most likely outcome.

feed.title: TSMC profit surges 61% to record high fueled by AI chip demand
feed.summary: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company (TSMC) reported a record 61% year-on-year surge in second-quarter profit, beating estimates, primarily driven by strong demand for AI chips. The company forecast a significant revenue increase for the third quarter and projected full-year 2025 revenue to rise by about 30% in U.S. dollar terms. Despite robust AI demand, TSMC acknowledged potential headwinds from U.S. trade policies, including possible new tariffs and existing export controls, as well as an appreciating Taiwan dollar and potential order cuts from other clients.

feed.title: TikTok integrates another streaming service into Add to Music App feature, with Anghami partnership
feed.summary: TikTok has partnered with Anghami, a leading streaming service in the Middle East and North Africa, to integrate it into its 'Add to Music App' feature. This allows users in the MENA region to directly save tracks discovered on TikTok to their Anghami music libraries. The collaboration follows the feature's successful global rollout in 2024, which has generated over a billion track saves, and coincides with Believe's expansion of Artist Services in the region and Nancy Ajram's new album release.

feed.title: Adams Diversified Equity Fund, Inc. Reports 7.8% Net Asset Value Return for H1 2025
feed.summary: Adams Diversified Equity Fund, Inc. reported a 7.8% net asset value return for the first half of 2025, outperforming the S&P 500 and Morningstar U.S. Large Blend category. The fund's market price return was 12.4%, with net assets of approximately $2.8 billion as of June 30, 2025, and significant holdings in technology companies like Microsoft and NVIDIA. The company also highlighted its consistent dividend history, targeting an annual distribution rate of at least 8% of NAV.

feed.title: Vornado Lands $450M to Refinance Penn 11
feed.summary: Vornado Realty Trust secured a $450 million CMBS loan to refinance Penn 11, a 1.2-million-square-foot office property, contributing $50 million in equity to cover a $500 million maturing debt. The five-year interest-only loan has a fixed rate of 6.35 percent. Major tenants include Apple, Sparc Group, and AMC Networks, with the building 97 percent occupied as of March.

feed.title: 'Overvalued' AI Stocks Like Nvidia, Microsoft May Trigger Market Crash Worse Than The Dot-Com Bubble, Economist Warns
feed.summary: A leading economist warns that the current AI stock frenzy, particularly in companies like Nvidia and Microsoft, represents a market overvaluation potentially worse than the dot-com bubble of 1999. Torsten Sløk of Apollo Global Management notes that the top 10 S&P 500 companies are more overvalued now than during the 1990s IT bubble, suggesting a major market crash could be triggered if these inflated promises are not met. Other prominent figures like Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella and billionaire Ray Dalio have also expressed similar concerns regarding a potential AI bubble.

feed.title: Forget SCHD? This New ETF Could Be the Future of Monthly Income
feed.summary: The article introduces the VistaShares Target 15 Berkshire Select Income ETF (OMAH) as a dynamic alternative to traditional dividend ETFs like SCHD. OMAH combines top Berkshire Hathaway holdings with a covered-call strategy, targeting a 15% annualized monthly yield, though primarily from options premiums. While it has a higher expense ratio and limits upside potential, it offers a compelling option for investors seeking enhanced monthly income in volatile markets.

feed.title: Corning staves off antitrust fine as EU regulators accept concessions to mobile phone makers
feed.summary: The EU antitrust regulators have accepted concessions from glass maker Corning, averting a potential fine. Corning agreed to waive exclusive dealing clauses with mobile phone makers and glass processing companies and to scrap purchasing clauses, ending an eight-month investigation. This ensures that original equipment manufacturers (OEMs) are not required to buy more than 50% of their demand from Corning, a measure valid for nine years.

feed.title: Consumer Electronics Market Trends, Growth Forecast & Opportunities 2025-2035
feed.summary: The global Consumer Electronics Market is projected to grow from USD 750.5 billion in 2024 to USD 1594.2 billion by 2035, driven by technological advancements, rising consumer disposable income, and the proliferation of connected devices. Key trends include the rise of smart homes, wearable tech, and eco-friendly electronics, while challenges involve rapid innovation cycles and supply chain disruptions. North America is a dominant market due to high purchasing power and early tech adoption, presenting numerous opportunities in emerging economies and sustainable product development.

feed.title: Studio LICO launches Mythic Item Obtained: Idle RPG globally
feed.summary: Studio LICO, a WEBTOON Entertainment Inc. game developer, has launched "Mythic Item Obtained: Idle RPG" globally in North America, Japan, and Taiwan. The mobile game adapts a popular webnovel and WEBTOON into an idle RPG format, featuring strategic progression and characters from the original series. Having already gained success in South Korea, the global launch includes a bonus code for in-game Gems and marks WEBTOON Entertainment's strategy to expand its IP across various media platforms.

feed.title: FDVV: Compelling Choice For Retirees That Want Income & Growth (NYSEARCA:FDVV)
feed.summary: The article highlights FDVV as an attractive ETF for retirees seeking a blend of dividend income and growth, particularly through its exposure to technology stocks. It suggests that FDVV offers higher price appreciation potential and better long-term total returns compared to traditional dividend ETFs like SCHD, while still providing a solid 3% yield and broad diversification. Despite minor drawbacks like tax efficiency and slightly lower dividend growth, its unique allocation makes it a defensive yet growth-oriented choice for retirement portfolios.

feed.title: Shareholder proposals on AI gain steam
feed.summary: Shareholder proposals concerning Artificial Intelligence (AI) are gaining traction in Canada, with investors pressing major companies to commit to responsible AI use. These proposals, exemplified by requests from groups like le mouvement d’éducation et de défense des actionnaires (MÉDAC), push companies to adopt voluntary codes of conduct or amend AI frameworks. While many proposals have not yet passed, they are proving effective in driving engagement and increased transparency from companies regarding their AI strategies and oversight.

feed.title: Fund Giants Put Faith in ‘Trump Put’ to Keep Stock Rally Rolling
feed.summary: Major asset managers like Invesco, Fidelity International, and JPMorgan Asset Management are increasing their investments in risk assets, especially US and Asian technology shares and emerging markets. This bullish strategy is based on the expectation that while Donald Trump may threaten economic disruption, he will ultimately pull back, allowing the stock rally to continue.

feed.title: Wireless Apple CarPlay and Android Auto: Where Are They Now?
feed.summary: This article details the increasing availability of wireless Apple CarPlay and Android Auto in 2025 model year vehicles, noting that these features are becoming more common, even in lower trim levels. It provides an extensive list of 2025 cars offering wireless smartphone integration and addresses common questions about the technology, including performance, connectivity, and battery life. The article also discusses potential challenges to the future of smartphone integration in cars, particularly due to automakers' interest in subscription services and data monetization.

feed.title: U.S. Computer Peripheral Market Size, and Growth Report, 2032
feed.summary: The U.S. computer peripheral market is projected to grow from USD 12.5 billion in 2024 to USD 94.3 billion by 2032, exhibiting an 8.3% CAGR from 2025. This growth is driven by increasing demand for advanced computing devices, the rise of remote and hybrid work models, and technological innovations like wireless connectivity and ergonomic designs. Wireless peripherals and input devices are key segments, with online distribution channels also seeing significant expansion.

feed.title: STRATEGIC INDUSTRY EXPERTS STEVE PROMNITZ AND BRETT LYNCH APPOINTED TO DRIVE U.S. EXPANSION STRATEGY
feed.summary: Resolution Minerals Limited has appointed Steve Promnitz and Brett Lynch as Senior Strategic Advisers to lead its U.S. critical minerals expansion strategy. These experts are known for building multi-billion dollar ASX-listed critical minerals companies and bring extensive experience in the U.S. regulatory, commercial, and investor landscapes. Their appointments are critical for Resolution's growth, especially with the U.S. government's increasing investment in critical metals and an upcoming drilling campaign at the Horse Heaven Project.

feed.title: Apollo Global Chief Economist Warns AI Bubble Worse Than Dot-Com Era, Predicts Looming Crash
feed.summary: Apollo Global Management's chief economist, Torsten Sløk, warns that the current AI boom constitutes a bubble even worse than the dot-com era, with leading AI companies having higher price-to-earnings ratios than tech firms in the 1990s. Sløk's analysis shows market gains are heavily concentrated in AI stocks, raising concerns about the sustainability of these valuations and predicting a potential market crash. Other industry figures share concerns that the AI boom could represent a financial bubble, with consequences potentially exceeding the early 2000s tech bust.

feed.title: Texas Instruments Stock Slumps on Soft Profit Forecast
feed.summary: Texas Instruments shares dropped in extended trading after the company issued a soft profit forecast for the third quarter, below analyst consensus. Despite this, the company reported stronger-than-expected second-quarter revenue and net income. Texas Instruments also announced significant investment plans for U.S. chip production.

feed.title: Richard Orrell’ Top Picks for July 22, 2025
feed.summary: Richard Orrell, Portfolio Manager at Croft Financial Group, shared his top ETF picks for July 22, 2025, which include the Invesco NASDAQ 100 Index ETF, Global X Artificial Intelligence Semiconductor Index ETF, and iShares Core S&P 500 Index ETF (CAD – Hedged). He noted that the market has largely priced in tariff-related news, and anticipates a downward trend in U.S. interest rates and a weakening U.S. dollar. Orrell also highlighted the strong long-term growth potential of artificial intelligence and suggested using any market softness at the end of July as an opportunity to increase equity exposure.

feed.title: What Is Apple CarPlay?
feed.summary: Apple CarPlay is a car feature that allows iPhone users to access certain apps through their vehicle's infotainment system, including navigation, communication, and music apps. It enhances safety by minimizing smartphone interaction while driving and has become a standard feature in many new cars, with wireless connectivity becoming increasingly common. Apple is also releasing an updated version, CarPlay Ultra, in 2025, which aims to make the feature more visually customizable and user-friendly.

feed.title: 三年年化超14%，SPYV ETF表現有何秘密?
feed.summary: SPYV ETF, which tracks the S&P 500 Value Index, has achieved over 14% annualized returns over the past three and five years, making it an attractive option for investors seeking stable, long-term growth in U.S. value stocks. The ETF focuses on undervalued, large-cap blue-chip stocks with a low expense ratio of 0.04%. Its strategic allocation across information technology, finance, and healthcare, along with defensive sectors, contributes to its balanced performance.

feed.title: WEBTOON Hit Webnovel Mythic Item Obtained Gets Mobile Game Release
feed.summary: Studio LICO, a part of WEBTOON Entertainment Inc., has globally launched its mobile game, Mythic Item Obtained: Idle RPG, in North America, Japan, and Taiwan. Based on the popular webnovel and webcomic, the game features idle gameplay and strategic battles, bringing the original series' immersive storytelling and characters to a wider audience. This global release follows its successful Korean debut where it topped popularity charts on both Google Play and the Apple App Store, maintaining an impressive 4.6-star rating.

feed.title: XLK vs. ARKK: Which Tech ETF Should Young Investors Buy for Growth?
feed.summary: This article compares two technology ETFs, the Technology Sector SPDR Fund (XLK) and Cathie Wood's Ark Innovation ETF (ARKK), to help young investors choose for growth. The XLK offers passive management and heavy concentration in mega-cap tech like Nvidia, Microsoft, and Apple, while ARKK provides active management and exposure to often volatile, disruptive tech companies. The author suggests ARKK might outperform XLK in the coming year for those comfortable with volatility and seeking "the next big thing," but advises against being all-in on ARKK or the tech sector.

feed.title: Comcast’s Xfinity Unveils StreamStore: A Smarter, Simpler Way to Discover, Manage and Enjoy Entertainment
feed.summary: Xfinity has launched StreamStore, a new platform designed to simplify the discovery, purchase, and management of streaming apps and channels for both Xfinity TV and Internet customers. This service offers a curated experience, allows for consolidated billing, and includes exclusive bundles like StreamSaver. StreamStore will also be available on Xfinity.com, expanding options for managing streaming services and other Xfinity accounts.

The page also features trending news, community discussions, and an interactive poll on market sentiment.

feed.title: Imax Reports Record Q2 Revenue, Re-Releasing Brad Pitt’s ‘F1: The Movie’ on Aug. 8
feed.summary: Imax reported its highest North American quarterly box office revenue ever in Q2, reaching $143 million, attributed to a record number of "Filmed for Imax" releases like "Sinners," "Mission: Impossible — The Final Reckoning," and "F1 The Movie." The company plans to re-release Apple Films' "F1: The Movie" on August 8, which has already grossed over $466 million globally. Imax anticipates achieving a record $1.2 billion in global box office for 2025.

feed.title: STMicroelectronics Shares Slump After Tesla Supplier Warns of Uncertainty
feed.summary: STMicroelectronics, a supplier to Tesla and Apple, saw its shares slump after forecasting another quarter of lower chip sales and warning about uncertainty due to President Trump’s tariffs. The company expects third-quarter revenue to be down 2.5% on year, with its gross margin also taking a hit. This follows a second quarter where sales were down more than 14% year-on-year.

feed.title: Billionaire Terry Smith Sold Fundsmith's Entire Stake in Apple and 11X'd His Position in a Company Whose Addressable Market Can Hit $149 Billion by 2032
feed.summary: Billionaire investor Terry Smith, known as the "Warren Buffett of Britain," has liquidated Fundsmith's entire stake in Apple (AAPL) and significantly increased his fund's position in Zoetis (ZTS) by over 11 times. Smith's decision to sell Apple is attributed to profit-taking after substantial gains and concerns over Apple's increasingly high valuation and lack of net income growth despite aggressive share buybacks. Conversely, he heavily invested in Zoetis, an animal health company, due to the stable growth of the global animal health market, Zoetis's market leadership, and its reasonable valuation.

feed.title: T-Mobile Launches Its Cellular Starlink Service: What You Need to Know
feed.summary: T-Mobile is officially launching its cellular Starlink service, "T-Satellite," making satellite connectivity available to smartphone users in dead zones for a monthly fee after a free beta period. While T-Mobile subscribers on premium plans get it free, others, including users on rival carriers, can subscribe for $10 per month, primarily for SMS and location sharing, with MMS and select app data support rolling out soon. The service functions by utilizing Starlink satellites as orbiting cell towers and is aimed at people in rural or remote areas, with plans to expand to 911 texting and international markets.

feed.title: Earnings Calendar (28.07.2025): Apple, Amazon, Meta Platforms, Microsoft
feed.summary: The article details the upcoming earnings reports from major tech companies Apple, Amazon, Meta Platforms, and Microsoft, set for July 28, 2025. This earnings season is crucial as Big Tech drives US stock market valuations, with investor focus intensified after reduced trade war risks. Beyond tech giants, earnings from Boeing, Procter & Gamble, Visa, and Mastercard will also be closely watched for insights into various market segments.

feed.title: Trademark Claims Against Peloton Over Use of ‘Bike+’ Fail
feed.summary: The Ninth U.S. Circuit Court of Appeals upheld a summary judgment in favor of Peloton Interactive Inc., ruling that trademark infringement claims over the use of "Peloton Bike+" failed. The court found that consumer confusion between Peloton's product and World Champ Tech LLC's "BIKE+" app was unlikely, despite both offering biking apps, because users are savvy enough to notice the producer's name alongside the icon in online stores. Although some factors initially favored the plaintiff, other factors, such as the distinct appearance of the apps in the Apple App Store and the purchasing process, overwhelmingly favored Peloton.

feed.title: Kilroy Realty Sells Bay Area Campus for $365M
feed.summary: Kilroy Realty has agreed to sell its Mathilda Campus, a three-building office and R&D complex in Sunnyvale, Calif., for $365 million. This transaction, set to close in late Q3, is one of Silicon Valley's largest this year and includes tenants like LinkedIn and Apple. The sale highlights a pickup in Silicon Valley office activity, with the region surpassing previous years' investment volumes in the first half of the year.

feed.title: Perfect Corp. Reports Unaudited Financial Results for the Three Months and the Six Months Ended June 30, 2025
feed.summary: Perfect Corp. (NYSE: PERF) announced its unaudited financial results for the three and six months ended June 30, 2025. The company reported increased revenue driven by mobile app and web services subscriptions, despite a decrease in net income for the three-month period and a slight decrease in adjusted net income for the six-month period. Perfect Corp. reiterated its full-year 2025 revenue guidance, projecting 13.0% to 14.5% year-over-year growth, emphasizing continued investment in generative AI and B2C app growth.

feed.title: Perfect Corp. Reports Unaudited Financial Results for the Three Months and the Six Months Ended June 30, 2025
feed.summary: Perfect Corp. announced its unaudited financial results for the three and six months ended June 30, 2025, showing increased revenue driven by mobile app and web services subscriptions, particularly from generative AI features. While enterprise segment growth remains strategic, the B2C segment is the primary revenue driver. The company reaffirms its full-year 2025 revenue guidance of 13.0% to 14.5% year-over-year.

feed.title: Lawsuit accuses Apple Pay of taking unauthorized subscription payments
feed.summary: A class-action lawsuit has been filed against Apple Pay and DoorDash, alleging deceptive practices regarding unauthorized subscription payments. The plaintiff, Kristine Divney, claims she was charged $9.99 for a DashPass subscription without her consent or knowledge, despite not having an account with DoorDash. The suit suggests a pattern of similar unauthorized charges affecting numerous users, challenging Apple Pay's security claims and DoorDash's billing practices.

feed.title: DTE Energy to supply 3GW electricity to Big Tech data centres
feed.summary: DTE Energy is currently in discussions to provide over 3GW of electricity to Big Tech data centers, with plans to finalize the first contract by the end of 2025. The utility is also in preliminary talks to supply an additional 4GW and intends to leverage existing infrastructure, build new battery storage, and potentially construct new natural gas-fired power generation plants to meet the soaring demand driven by AI expansion. This move comes as US power companies face increasing requests for energy from data-intensive tech facilities, leading DTE to increase infrastructure investments.

feed.title: PMG Partners with Genius Sports to Pioneer Sports Advertising Innovation
feed.summary: PMG and Genius Sports have announced a strategic partnership to revolutionize sports advertising. PMG will become a founding agency partner of Genius Sports' FANHub platform, focusing on next-generation ad formats, measurement innovation, and early access to emerging technologies for its clients. The collaboration aims to leverage AI-driven insights and immersive experiences to enhance fan engagement and provide measurable business outcomes for brands in the sports advertising space.

feed.title: Qualcomm Q3 revenue up 10% as it expands into smart glasses and AI computers
feed.summary: Qualcomm's fiscal Q3 revenue increased by 10% to $10.4 billion, driven by growth in automotive, internet-connected segments, and smartphone chipset sales, as well as expansions into AI-backed personal computers and smart glasses. The company is actively diversifying into data center chips, including a $2.4 billion acquisition of Alphawave Semi and advanced discussions with a leading hyperscaler customer, aiming to reduce reliance on the smartphone market and capitalize on the AI boom. Qualcomm also faces the challenge of Apple increasingly using its own modem products but expects handset revenues to grow from Q3 to Q4.

LLC, focuses on growth opportunities in sectors like Electronic Technology and Technology Services, while providing a substantial dividend yield of 10.24%.

feed.title: Wuxi AppTec joins funding rush as appetite grows for China’s biotech
feed.summary: Wuxi AppTec, Asia's largest provider of contract pharmaceutical research, has raised US$981 million through a sale of additional shares in Hong Kong to fund its global expansion and capacity construction. This move follows a trend of other Chinese novel drug developers tapping capital markets, driven by bullish appetite from mainland investors for Hong Kong-listed biotech and pharmaceutical firms. The company sold 73.8 million new shares to institutional investors at a discount, with the stock experiencing a slight dip after the offering.

The article also covers key statistics, historical returns, and answers frequently asked questions about the ETF.

feed.title: University of Michigan startup Ambiq goes public
feed.summary: University of Michigan startup Ambiq, co-founded by Professors David Blaauw, Dennis Sylvester, and alumnus Dr. Scott Hanson, recently went public on the New York Stock Exchange under the ticker symbol AMBQ. The company's ultra-efficient chips, developed from NSF-funded research, are used in wearables like Fitbit, Garmin, and Whoop, and Ambiq is now expanding its mission to run AI models on small devices. This move marks a significant achievement in commercializing research that began in a university lab, solving the power bottleneck for AI in various compact electronics.

feed.title: JEPQ: This Looks Like The Right Moment To Own Nasdaq With Income
feed.summary: The article advocates for JEPQ, a covered call ETF, as a suitable investment for current market conditions, especially for those seeking income from Nasdaq holdings. It highlights JEPQ's diversified nature and lower downside risk compared to single-stock strategies. The author suggests JEPQ is best used as a tactical complement to a portfolio when moderate returns are expected from the Nasdaq.

feed.title: Billionaire Warren Buffett Sold 39% of Berkshire's Stake in Bank of America and Is Piling Into an Industry Leader That's Gained Almost 48,000% Since Its IPO
feed.summary: Warren Buffett, CEO of Berkshire Hathaway, has significantly reduced his stake in Bank of America, selling 39% of his position over nine months. This divestment is likely influenced by potential future corporate tax rate hikes and the bank's sensitivity to falling interest rates. Concurrently, Buffett has been increasing Berkshire's investment in Pool Corp. (POOL), a company with a strong track record and a predictable razor-and-blade business model benefiting from recurring maintenance sales and digitization efforts.

feed.title: Differentiating Large From Small: Firm Size and Exposure to Trade Tensions
feed.summary: This article by J.P. Morgan explores how firm size affects exposure to trade tensions, concluding that smaller businesses are more vulnerable due to limited trading partners, greater reliance on China, and difficulty passing costs to consumers. The author, Vinny Amaru, suggests that these factors contribute to slower economic growth, while large-cap equities are better positioned to weather ongoing trade challenges. The article recommends favoring U.S. large-cap equities over smaller-cap U.S. stocks in the near term.

feed.title: Apple increases U.S. commitment to $600 billion, announces ambitious program

feed.summary: Apple Original Films' "F1 The Movie," starring Brad Pitt, is returning to IMAX theaters starting August 8, 2025, due to popular demand. The re-release and its successful soundtrack underscore its significant cultural and box office impact in 2025.

feed.title: People Moves: Aniket Kulkarni, Apple
feed.summary: Aniket Kulkarni, previously Head of Sourcing for Dairy at Sysco for 13 years, has joined Apple as Global Supply Manager. Kulkarni is known for his expertise in strategic sourcing and supply chain management, having orchestrated procurement initiatives and driven significant cost reductions at Sysco. feed.title: Apple, Corning to manufacture all iPhone, Apple Watch cover glass in Kentucky

The initiative will also establish an Apple-Corning Innovation Center and increase Corning's Kentucky workforce by 50 percent.

feed.title: TSMC gets a reprieve from Trump's 100% chip tariffs thanks to its U.S. factories, Taiwan says
feed.summary: Taiwanese chipmaking giant TSMC will be exempt from former U.S. President Donald Trump's proposed 100% tariff on semiconductor chips. This exemption is due to TSMC's factories located in the United States, as confirmed by Taiwan's National Development Council chief Liu Chin-ching. feed.title: General Motors, Hyundai to develop vehicles together amid China EV competition

While the partnership seeks to create synergies and leverage each company's strengths, some analysts question its effectiveness in challenging the low-priced dominance of Chinese rivals in the EV market. The piece also notes Apple CEO Tim Cook's recent announcement about increasing investment in US manufacturing, with Corning's Kentucky facility producing glass for all new iPhones and Apple Watches.

feed.title: Trump demands resignation of Intel CEO over China ties

feed.title: CAFC Vacates Non-Infringement Ruling for Apple Due to District Court Error in Striking Expert Opinion
feed.summary: The U.S. Court of Appeals for the Federal Circuit (CAFC) vacated a district court's summary judgment of non-infringement for Apple, Inc., finding that the lower court improperly struck an expert opinion from Taction Technology, Inc. The CAFC ruled that the district court's interpretation of Local Patent Rule 3.1(c) to include an unwritten "how" requirement was arbitrary and unsupported by the rule's plain language. Additionally, the CAFC found the striking of the expert's testimony regarding claim construction to be an abuse of discretion, although it agreed with the district court on a specific claim limitation interpretation from the patent prosecution history.

feed.title: 100% US Tariffs on Semiconductors, with Exemptions — Is TSMC Safe?｜Industry｜2025-08-13｜web only
feed.summary: Former U.S. President Donald Trump announced a 100% tariff on semiconductors, but with exemptions for companies investing in American manufacturing. This policy, described as a "carrot and stick" approach, caused initial surges in stock prices for companies like TSMC, Apple, and Nvidia, as they have significant U.S. investments. While major players like TSMC and Samsung anticipate exemption due to their existing commitments, the policy's complete details, including effective dates and country-of-origin definitions, are still pending, leaving a degree of uncertainty.

feed.title: Coherent sells aerospace and defense business for $400M
feed.summary: Coherent has announced the sale of its aerospace and defense business to private equity firm Advent International for $400 million. The sale aims to streamline Coherent's portfolio, reduce its $3.5 billion debt, and improve profitability, despite the divested unit generating $200 million in sales with lower profit margins. Concurrently, Coherent reported record-breaking annual sales of $5.81 billion, driven by a 50% increase in data center-related products, and plans to start production on the world's first six-inch indium phosphide (InP) line at its Sherman facility, though its stock dropped nearly 20% on a disappointing outlook.

feed.title: Apple accidentally leaked its own top-secret hardware in software code—and it looks like we're getting a slew of products across 7 categories
feed.summary: Apple appears to have inadvertently revealed a range of unannounced hardware through internal identifiers found in publicly shared software code. These leaks suggest next-generation chips across seven product categories: iPhone, iPad, Apple Watch, Mac, Vision Pro, HomePod mini, and Apple TV. While Apple has not officially announced these products, the information provides concrete clues about the company's future roadmap and upcoming technological advancements.

feed.title: Applied Materials shares sink 10% on light forecast amid macroeconomic uncertainties

Despite beating fiscal third-quarter earnings and revenue estimates, the semiconductor equipment company projects a decline in fourth-quarter revenue due to capacity digestion in China and non-linear demand from leading-edge customers. The company also faces challenges with a large backlog of export license applications and the potential impact of Trump administration tariffs.

feed.title: Cisco Earnings: We Raise Our AI Forecast After Strong Results and Guidance

While AI is a key growth driver, Morningstar notes that valuation is challenging at current levels.

feed.title: BOE Faces U.S. Export Threat After Ruling in Samsung Trade Secret Case
feed.summary: A U.S. International Trade Commission (ITC) preliminary ruling found China’s BOE Technology Group guilty of misappropriating trade secrets from Samsung Display Co. Ltd., potentially restricting BOE's exports of high-end OLED display screens to the U.S. The ruling, issued mid-July, sided with Samsung's allegations that BOE did not independently develop or obtain the disputed OLED technology from public sources. Despite this, BOE is a significant supplier of iPhone panels, having recently overtaken LG Display in market share.

feed.title: Applied Materials sinks 14% on weak guidance due to China demand
feed.summary: Applied Materials shares plunged 14% after the semiconductor equipment maker issued weak guidance due to demand pressures in China, falling short of revenue and earnings per share estimates. This weakness is attributed to macroeconomic pressures, trade issues, and a slowdown in spending from China clients after a period of rapid manufacturing. Despite the weak guidance, the company did top its third-quarter earnings and revenue estimates.

feed.title: Instagram’s “Friend Map”: experts warn of risks
feed.summary: Meta has introduced "Friend Map" on Instagram, a new feature allowing users to see friends' locations and common meeting points. Security experts at Check Point warn of significant risks, including potential for stalking, harassment, and privacy breaches due to the centralized, unencrypted storage of sensitive location data. Users are advised to take protective measures like restricting location access and regularly reviewing follower lists to mitigate these dangers.

feed.title: Applied Materials stock falls on profit, sales forecasts

The company expects lower revenue and profit for the fiscal fourth quarter due to reduced demand from Chinese customers and delays in export approvals. Despite this, Applied Materials is participating in Apple Inc.'s "American Manufacturing Program" and remains optimistic about long-term demand for computing power.

feed.title: Masimo Challenges CBP’s Ex Parte Order Allowing Apple to Restore Infringing Pulse Oximetry Function
feed.summary: Masimo Corporation has filed a complaint against U.S. Customs and Border Protection (CBP) and U.S. government officials, challenging an ex parte ruling that permits Apple to restore pulse oximetry functionality to its smartwatches. This functionality was previously found to infringe on Masimo's patents by the U.S. International Trade Commission (USITC), leading to a limited exclusion order. Masimo alleges the CBP "inexplicably reversed course" through an ex parte proceeding, questioning the agency's justification for such a ruling and its timing given Apple's recent significant investment announcements in U.S. manufacturing.
